title: Implement Subnetworks for Publicly Accessible Systems
description: Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are physically or logically separated from internal networks.

checks.name: Network Segmentation Architecture Documented
checks.purpose: Verify network architecture diagrams and documentation show DMZ/subnet separation of publicly accessible systems — architecture review only

checks.name: Network Profile is Domain (Internal Subnet)
checks.purpose: Confirm the workstation is on a Domain-authenticated network profile, indicating proper placement on an internal subnet rather than a public-facing segment

checks.name: Publicly Accessible Services Reside in Separate Subnet
checks.purpose: Verify that any publicly accessible services (web, mail, DNS) are hosted on separate DMZ subnets with firewall rules restricting access to internal systems — requires manual network review

checks.name: Network Segmentation Architecture Documented
checks.purpose: Verify documented network architecture shows DMZ separation of public-facing servers — architecture review only

checks.name: Network Profile is Domain (Internal Subnet)
checks.purpose: Confirm the server is on a Domain-authenticated network, indicating proper internal subnet placement separate from publicly accessible segments

checks.name: Publicly Accessible Services in Separate Subnet
checks.purpose: Verify public-facing services are separated from internal servers via DMZ — requires manual network architecture review

checks.name: Inter-Segment Firewall Rules Restrict DMZ to Internal Access
checks.purpose: Confirm firewall rules between the DMZ and internal network segments restrict access to only required flows — requires perimeter device review

checks.name: Network Segmentation Architecture Documented
checks.purpose: Verify documented network architecture shows subnet separation for publicly accessible systems — architecture review only

checks.name: System Has RFC-1918 Internal IP Address
checks.purpose: Verify the system's IP address is in an RFC-1918 private range, confirming placement on an internal subnet rather than a public-facing segment

checks.name: Publicly Accessible Services Separated from Internal Network
checks.purpose: Verify routing table and network interfaces confirm this system is not directly internet-routable — requires manual network review

checks.name: Network Segmentation Architecture Documented
checks.purpose: Verify documented network architecture shows subnet separation for publicly accessible systems — architecture review only

checks.name: System Has RFC-1918 Internal IP Address
checks.purpose: Verify the system's IP address is in an RFC-1918 private range, confirming placement on an internal subnet rather than a public-facing segment

checks.name: Publicly Accessible Services Separated from Internal Network
checks.purpose: Verify routing table and network interfaces confirm this system is not directly internet-routable — requires manual network review

remediation: Ensure all publicly accessible servers (web, mail, DNS) are placed in a DMZ subnet separated from the internal network by a firewall. Internal workstations and servers handling CUI must reside on internal subnets with no direct routing from public networks. Document the network architecture.
